ucs.title: [LLM] Evilnum KYC-lure: ZIP→LNK→wscript JS dropper chain (financial sector)
ucs.description: Hunts Evilnum's signature initial-access pattern where spearphishing links deliver a ZIP archive containing multiple LNK files masquerading as KYC docs (utility bills, drivers' licenses, credit-card scans, selfies). Opening any LNK launches wscript/cscript to run an embedded/extracted JS component (the 'Evilnum' JS dropper) that beacons out and stages More_eggs or PyVil. This combination of fintech/forex KYC lure + LNK + JS host is highly characteristic of G0120.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.zip") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
| eval kyc_lure=if(match(lower(file_name),"(utility|driver|licen[cs]e|credit|passport|selfie|scan|photo|kyc|proof|invoice|statement|id[_-])"),1,0)
| where kyc_lure=1
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","cmd.exe") AND (Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.jse*" OR Processes.process="*.wsf*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id _time
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename _time as script_time
]
| eval delta=abs(script_time-lastTime)
| where delta<=1800
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) ctime(script_time)
| table firstTime lastTime script_time dest user file_name file_path parent_process_name process_name process
ucs.rationale: Evilnum (ESET, Zscaler reporting) is one of very few crews that consistently weaponizes KYC-themed ZIPs full of LNKs as the initial-access primitive against forex/crypto/fintech firms, then pivots via wscript-hosted JavaScript stage-1. Generic LNK→wscript hunts are noisy; constraining on KYC-style filenames in user download paths within a 30-minute window of script execution yields a tight, actor-

ucs.title: [LLM] Evilnum hands-on-keyboard: legitimate RDP/RAT install + browser credential & cookie theft
ucs.description: Targets Evilnum's post-exploitation tradecraft of side-loading or installing legitimate remote-access software (TeamViewer, AnyDesk, ScreenConnect) on a freshly compromised fintech host and then immediately accessing browser Login Data / Cookies stores (often via PyVil, More_eggs, or interactive operator activity) to harvest broker / trading-platform sessions.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as ratFirst max(_time) as ratLast values(Processes.process) as rat_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Service.exe","AnyDesk.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","tvnserver.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as credFirst from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*\\Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*\\Cookies*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\cookies.sqlite" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\*\\Login Data*") AND Filesystem.action IN ("read","copy","opened") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | search process_name!="chrome.exe" AND process_name!="msedge.exe" AND process_name!="firefox.exe"
]
| eval delta=abs(credFirst-ratFirst)
| where delta<=3600
| convert ctime(ratFirst) ctime(ratLast) ctime(credFirst)
| table ratFirst credFirst dest user process_name rat_cmd file_path
ucs.kql:
let rat_window = 1h;
let rat_procs = dynamic(["teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","anydesk.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","tvnserver.exe"]);
let rat =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where tolower(FileName) in (rat_procs)
    | summarize RatFirst=min(Timestamp), RatCmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,5) by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, RatTool=FileName;
let credtheft =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
          or InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe")
    | where FolderPath has_any (
        @"\Google\Chrome\User Data\",
        @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\",
        @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\")
    | where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","cookies.sqlite","logins.json","key4.db")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe")
    | project DeviceId, CredTime=Timestamp, CredFile=FileName, CredPath=FolderPath, CredProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, CredCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
rat
| join kind=inner credtheft on DeviceId
| where CredTime between (RatFirst - 30m .. RatFirst + rat_window)
| project RatFirst, CredTime, DeviceName, AccountName, RatTool, RatCmds, CredProc, CredFile, CredPath, CredCmd
ucs.rationale: Evilnum's reporting (ESET 2020, Zscaler PyVil 2020) consistently shows the operators dropping legitimate remote-access binaries to avoid AV attention, then pivoting straight to browser credential and trading-cookie theft (the actor's profit center is hijacked broker/forex/crypto sessions). Joining a fresh RAT install with non-browser-process access to Chrome/Edge/Firefox Login Data + Cookies insid
